title.en: I don't buy it! 난 안 믿어! 그 말 못 믿겠어!
title.ko: 난 안 믿어! 그 말 못 믿겠어!

description.en: When you can't believe someone's absurd claims or excuses! Practice expressing your disbelief with 난 안 믿어 and 그 말 못 믿겠어.
description.ko: 상대방의 터무니없는 주장이나 변명을 믿을 수 없을 때! 난 안 믿어와 그 말 못 믿겠어를 사용해 의심스러운 마음을 리얼하게 표현해 봅니다.

tags: Reaction. Disbelief. Doubt. Skepticism. Daily Life

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world! Welcome back. You are listening to... "Real Reaction!" I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 오늘따라 피부가 되게 좋아 보여요. 비결이 뭐예요?
content.translation: John. your skin looks really good today. What is the secret?

content.speaker: John
content.text: I have a special routine. I only wash my face with milk from the Swiss Alps.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 에이 난 안 믿어! 우유로 세수하면 얼굴에 냄새나지 않을까요?
content.translation: Haha. Hey. I don't believe it! Wouldn't your face smell if you wash it with milk?

content.speaker: John
content.text: It's true! And I drink five liters of water every hour too.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그 말 못 믿겠어! 물을 그렇게 많이 마시면 화장실 가느라 방송 녹음도 못 할걸요?
content.translation: Haha. I don't buy that! If you drink that much water. you wouldn't even be able to record the show because you'd be going to the bathroom.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay. okay. You caught me. I just got a good night's sleep.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 난 안 믿어. This is very direct. right.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 어떤 사실이나 상황 자체가 거짓말 같을 때 짧고 굵게 말할 수 있는 표현이에요.
content.translation: Right. It is an expression you can say shortly and strongly when a fact or situation itself feels like a lie.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 난 안 믿어.
content.translation: I don't believe it.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그리고 그 말 못 믿겠어 라는 말은 상대방이 방금 한 구체적인 말이나 내용에 신뢰가 가지 않을 때 써요.
content.translation: And the phrase 'Geu mal mot mit-get-eo' is used when you don't trust the specific 'words' or 'content' someone just said.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그 말 못 믿겠어.
content.translation: I don't buy those words.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What about when I want to say "Stop kidding."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐 거짓말 마! 라고 해보세요. 상대방의 장난 섞인 거짓말을 멈추게 할 때 아주 좋거든요.
content.translation: Haha. In that case. try saying 'Geo-jit-mal ma'. It's great for stopping someone's playful lies.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay. let's try a situation. Imagine I promised to bring you a famous cake. but I showed up empty-handed.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존. 제가 기다리던 그 케이크는 어디 있어요?
content.translation: John. where is that cake I was waiting for?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 설마 잊어버린 건 아니죠?
content.translation: You didn't forget. did you?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Oh. Mina. I really bought it! But on my way here. a giant eagle flew down and snatched it from my hands!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 난 안 믿어! 서울 하늘에 그렇게 큰 독수리가 어디 있어요?
content.translation: Haha. I don't believe it! Where is there such a big eagle in the sky of Seoul?

content.speaker: John
content.text: It's true! It was a very hungry eagle!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 진짜 그 말 못 믿겠어! 차라리 그냥 배고파서 존이 다 먹었다고 하세요!
content.translation: Haha. I really don't buy that! Just say you ate it all because you were hungry. John!

content.speaker: John
content.text: You're too smart. Mina.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Knowing these reactions will help you handle those "too good to be true" stories.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 너무 어이없는 핑계를 들었을 때 당황하지 말고 오늘 배운 표현들로 대답해 보세요!
content.translation: Right. When you hear an absurd excuse. don't be flustered and answer with the expressions we learned today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "I don't buy it" reactions.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 난 안 믿어!
content.translation: I don't believe it!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그 말 못 믿겠어!
content.translation: I don't buy that!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 거짓말 마!
content.translation: Stop lying!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 말도 안 되는 소리 하지 마!
content.translation: Don't talk nonsense!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그걸 믿으라고?
content.translation: You expect me to believe that?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 누군가 말도 안 되는 허풍을 떨면 시원하게 외쳐보세요!
content.translation: Everyone. if someone tells an absurd tall tale today. shout it out coolly!

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 저는 존의 말을 믿어요. (I believe what John says.)
거짓말하는 건 나쁜 습관이에요. (Lying is a bad habit.)
늦은 것에 대해 핑계를 대지 마세요. (Don't make excuses for being late.)
그 사람의 이야기는 허풍이 심해요. (That person's stories are full of bluffs.)
우리는 서로 신뢰하는 사이예요. (We are in a relationship where we trust each other.)
수상한 점이 많아서 의심이 가요. (There are many suspicious points. so I have doubts.)
